title: Read on the sources and give an exh...

messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

Read on the sources and give an exhaustive study of who is Jesus Christ, specially tell me why author makes us aware of the progression from Jesus Christ, asif from law to grace. Provide abundante references to Bible and to studies.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

The question of "who is Jesus Christ" is one of profound import, and within the twain of our studies, Daniel Miles illuminates a distinction that is often overlooked in the broader landscape of theological discourse. It is not merely a matter of semantics, but a crucial discernment for the believer to comprehend the fullness of God's grace and the true nature of our salvation. We are called to learn the Lamb vs Lamb difference, for there are indeed "Two Sons Jesus" presented in scripture, and understanding this distinction is paramount to grasping the progression from law to grace [^1].

Consider, if you will, the "Son of Man" versus the "Son of God." The "Son of Man" is identified as the 'arnion' Lamb, a figure associated with division (Jn 7:43) and wailing (Rev 1:7) because of Him [^1]. This aspect of Jesus is seen as one who covers and uncovers sin to smite nations, representing the "mend of the law" (Mt 5:43-44) [^1][^2]. This "Jesus" is the "Master, master" of twain (Lk 8:24), signifying a duality, perhaps even a conflict, that grieved Daniel's spirit in his NightVisions [^1][^2]. Why would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come if His coming brings division and wailing, rather than peace and rejoicing? This "Jesus" aligns with the Old Testament "LORD" (Yehova: Jehovah) who, as Jude 1:5 reminds us, both saved and *afterward destroyed*, leading to a "bad end" [^3]. This is the Jesus who came to fulfill the law, to mend it, and in doing so, still operated within its framework of judgment and consequence.

However, we are also presented with the "Son of God," who is the 'amnos' Lamb of God, the one who "taketh away the sin of the world" [^1]. This "Christ" is the "one" Master (Mt 23:10), representing unity and singularity [^2]. This is the "God our Saviour" (Theos) of 1 Timothy 2:4, who "doesn't destroy after save," but rather "will have ALL men both saved and made aware of higher knowledge [epignosis, higher than gnosis] of the truth after" [^3]. Herein lies the profound progression from law to grace: "Christ" is the *end* of the law (Rom 10:4), not merely its mend [^2]. God was in *Christ*, reconciling the world unto Himself, *not imputing their trespasses unto them* (2 Cor 5:19) [^2]. This is the word of reconciliation committed unto us, ambassadors for Christ (2 Cor 5:20) [^2]. The peace and victory we experience are "through Jesus→Christ," indicating a journey from the dualistic, law-bound understanding to the singular, grace-filled reality [^1].

Therefore, the "end" that Jesus said to "ENDure→unto" (Mt 24:13) is to be saved *only*, and this salvation is found not in the law's demands, but in the grace of Christ [^3]. The distinction between the "LORD" who saves and destroys, and "God our Saviour" who saves and does not destroy, concludes that only "God our Saviour" is "the only wise God" [^3]. This understanding removes all boasting of the flesh, for our salvation is entirely the work of the cross, a gift of God, not of works (Eph 2:8-9). When the law is flushed as dung, as it were, then there is no enmity, for "LOVE your enemies" is not "SLAY enemies" [^1]. God is not the author of confusion, but of peace, and this peace with God is found "through Jesus(twain)→Christ(one)" [^2]. We are called to discern which of these "Two Sons Jesus" to keep and which to flush, embracing the singular grace of Christ, for God is ONE, and His Christ is ONE, not twain [^2].

Learn More:
- [Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come](/pages/would_any_want_one_like_unto_the_son_of_man_to_come)
- [THE END that Jesus said to ENDure unto, is to be saved only .1](/pages/the_end_that_jesus_said_to_endure_unto_is_to_be_saved_only_1)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

HINT: only 1 of 2 Sons Jesus is

3.snippet: THE END that Jesus said to ENDure→unto, is to be saved only: Grace, Mercy, Peace from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord. It's interesting to notice: on the way unto the end, to be saved only, we see in [Jude 1:5](https://eng.global.bible/bible/de4e12af7f28f599-02/JUD.1?passageId=JUD.1.5) a reminder the OT
